quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! Their wings rotate in a full circle, allowing them to hover in place and even fly upside down for short distances. These tiny acrobats can beat their wings up to 80 times per second.

short_read.title: The Secret Language of Flowers in Victorian Times
short_read.teaser: Did you know that in the 1800s, people sent coded messages through flower bouquets? Each bloom had a special meaning, turning gardens into poetry.

medium_read.title: The Return of Sea Otters to the Pacific Northwest
medium_read.teaser: Once nearly extinct, sea otters are making a remarkable comeback along the Northwest coast. These charming creatures are not just adorable — they're essential guardians of the kelp forests.

long_read.title: Ellis Island: Gateway to America's Immigrant Stories
long_read.teaser: Between 1892 and 1954, over 12 million immigrants passed through Ellis Island, each carrying dreams of a new life. Their stories shaped the fabric of America in ways we still feel today.

port_angeles.title: Ancient Trees at Lake Crescent
port_angeles.description: Just a short drive from Port Angeles, Lake Crescent's Marymere Falls Trail takes you through a cathedral of old-growth forest, with Douglas firs and Western red cedars over 300 years old. The easy 1.8-mile loop trail leads to a stunning 90-foot waterfall — a perfect summer morning outing with cool shade and fresh mountain air.

puzzle_1.question: A newborn giant panda weighs about the same as a stick of butter — only about ___ ounces!
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! It's amazing how these tiny babies grow into 200-pound adults!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! They weigh about 4 ounces — smaller than most cell phones!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: They're incredibly tiny — just 4 ounces! That's about 1/900th the size of their mother!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Despite being so tiny at birth, giant pandas grow rapidly on their mother's rich milk, which is five times more nutritious than cow's milk. By their first birthday, they weigh about 45 pounds!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Igen! Pillangó is butterfly — such a beautiful, dancing word for a dancing creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: It's 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound like the flutter of wings? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and napraforgó means sunflower!)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The Hungarian word 'pillangó' is thought to come from the word for 'fleeting' or 'fluttering,' perfectly capturing the delicate movement of these lovely insects. Hungary is home to over 180 species of butterflies!

puzzle_3.question: What color is a giraffe's tongue?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Their tongues are blue-black, which scientists think helps protect them from sunburn since they spend so much time reaching up to eat!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Their tongues are actually blue-black! This dark color acts like natural sunscreen, protecting them as they spend up to 12 hours a day munching on leaves in the hot African sun.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: A giraffe's tongue can be up to 20 inches long and is incredibly tough and dexterous, allowing them to strip leaves from thorny acacia trees without getting hurt. The dark pigmentation may also prevent sun damage during their long feeding sessions.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
